eemr 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

Compute a module x sample activity matrix

module_activity.Rd

For each expression module returned by eem_search, computes its "activity" in each sample as the mean expression of its module genes (the moduleGenes column), giving a matrix with one row per module and one column per sample.

Usage

module_activity(expr, result, normalize = TRUE, pvalueCutoff = 6)

Arguments

expr

the same numeric matrix (genes x samples, with gene rownames) that was passed to eem_search.

result

a data.frame returned by eem_search (must have id and moduleGenes columns).

normalize

standardize each gene (row of expr) to mean 0, sd 1 before averaging (default TRUE), matching the row-normalization eem_search() applies internally before searching for modules, so that genes on different expression scales contribute comparably to the module's activity score. Set to FALSE to average raw expression values instead.

pvalueCutoff

only include modules whose pvalue (-log10 scale, same convention as pvalue1Cutoff in eem_search) is at least this value. Default 6 (i.e. p < 1e-6). Set to NULL to include every module in result.

Value

a numeric matrix with one row per included module (named by result$id) and ncol(expr) columns (named by colnames(expr)).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
res <- eem_search(expr, geneSets)
activity <- module_activity(expr, res)
# only keep modules significant at p < 0.01 (-log10(0.01) = 2)
activity <- module_activity(expr, res, pvalueCutoff = -log10(0.01))
} # }
